pattern: Storing words in a trie lets a wildcard search branch into every child whenever it meets a dot, turning prefix-by-prefix matching into a bounded depth-first walk.
hints: Words share prefixes, so a character tree (trie) keeps lookups proportional to the word length rather than the number of stored words.
Insertion walks the tree creating missing child nodes and marks the final node as a complete word.
Search recurses character by character; a normal letter follows one child, while a dot must try every existing child at that position before failing.

edge_inputs.description: add three words then query exact, missing, and dotted patterns
single one-letter word matched by an exact letter and by a dot
a pattern shorter or longer than every stored word must not match
all dots match any stored word of that exact length only
searching before anything is added always fails

stress.description: random short lowercase words over a small alphabet with dotted and plain queries
stress.note: queries derive from added words so both matches and misses occur; at most 2 dots per query
